$comment: Layer B selection cases for REMIT vs the real GnuCOBOL harness image (file I/O stage 1 + WRITE ... FROM, desugared to MOVE + WRITE per examples/probes/writefrom.cbl). The legacy side runs the wrapper entrypoint: program output (ACCT_ID/ROWS) followed by the serialized remit.dat records (AMT/CHG/NET), one KV stream. Candidate B drops the desugared MOVE on the second WRITE and emits the stale record buffer (the AMT line again in place of CHG) - the classic WRITE FROM migration bug, caught on every case. Cases cover a typical advice, the charge half-cent tie, a charge rounding to zero, and zero and max amounts.

legacy.label: COBOL 85 REMIT (GnuCOBOL via harness image legacymind/legacy-remit, file-serializing wrapper)
